claim: Context: "Express-30" feature, 3-week sprint, retro on day 8 in prod
quote: This is the retro for the "Express-30" launch sprint — 30-minute delivery, three weeks of work, already eight days in prod.

claim: Nastya is at retro with the team for the first time
quote: Nastya's with us at retro for the first time

claim: Role and composition of the dev team
quote: Dima, backend team lead, three people on the team plus me.

claim: Keep: dailies strictly at 15 minutes
quote: Keep for me is that we did dailies strictly at 15 minutes, no dragging on.

claim: Igor insisted on the daily timebox
quote: Yeah, I insisted on the timebox, because last sprint the dailies were running to forty minutes.

claim: Keep: early alignment of the mockup with API constraints
quote: We locked the slot flow mockup in Figma with comments from Nastya on the API right away, that rarely goes so smoothly.

claim: Nastya confirms the early alignment was Lena's own initiative
quote: usually I find out about API constraints once the design is already done, but here Lena came to ask herself at the draft stage.

claim: Keep: cheat sheet for support 2 days before release
quote: From me — that cheat sheet for operators you sent two days before release.

claim: Nastya wrote the cheat sheet
quote: Well it was just three paragraphs about typical courier API timeout errors.

claim: Stop: scope locked only in the second week of a three-week sprint
quote: We only got the final feature scope in the second week of the sprint, even though the sprint was three weeks.

claim: Specific moment — Tuesday of the second week, new scope from Igor
quote: Tuesday of the second week, when Igor sent over the new scope adding courier notifications about address changes — that wasn't in the original spec.

claim: Stop: feature handed to support one day before release
quote: Another stop — handoff. We handed the feature to support for testing literally one day before release, Oleg didn't have time to run through the scenarios.

claim: Oleg got test-stand access Thursday evening, release was Friday
quote: I got access to the test environment Thursday evening, and the release was Friday afternoon.

claim: Consequence: 12 tickets about a slot showing as taken
quote: on day one alone we had twelve tickets about the slot showing "taken" even though the courier was free

claim: Stop: nightly job failed silently, no alert
quote: From me — the nightly slot recalculation job would sometimes fail silently, no alert, I'd only find out from Oleg's tickets in the morning.

claim: Job failure frequency — twice a week
quote: Right, that happened twice in the week after release.

claim: Try: lock the feature scope no later than day one of the sprint
quote: Try from me — lock the feature scope no later than day one of the sprint.

claim: Try: pre-launch checklist with support access 3 days ahead
quote: try number two — set up a pre-launch checklist that always includes support access at least three days ahead.

claim: Dima takes on the pre-launch checklist
quote: I'll take it, I already have a draft from the last project, I'll just add three items.

claim: Try: automatic Slack alerts for nightly jobs
quote: it'd be great if Slack alerts got set up automatically when a new job gets deployed, instead of by hand afterward.

claim: Facilitator notes no owner for the alerts item
quote: I hear that everyone agrees it's important, but nobody's picking it up.

claim: Facilitator logs the item as open with no owner
quote: noting it as an open item with no owner, we'll come back to it at the next retro.

claim: Try: formalize design review with backend by default
quote: I want to propose doing design review with Nastya at the draft stage by default, not just when I happen to remember to come and ask.

claim: Nastya caveats — success was a workload coincidence
quote: this time it happened by chance, we got lucky that I wasn't loaded up at that moment.

claim: Fork: Igor added courier notification to scope due to legal
quote: when Igor sent over the new scope adding courier notifications about address changes — that wasn't in the original spec

claim: Igor explains the addition via a legal change
quote: That was a change from legal, I couldn't ignore it.

claim: Fork: Dima takes on the pre-launch checklist right away, no pushback
quote: I'll take it, I already have a draft from the last project

claim: Fork: Nastya declines the alerts item due to workload
quote: I could look into it, but I won't have time in the next two weeks, I've got vacation plus another project.

claim: Igor proposes handing alerts to DevOps
quote: Maybe someone from DevOps? We do have a separate infrastructure team, don't we?

claim: Friction: Lena and Nastya discussed the deadline risk during a coffee break, not at the daily
quote: it felt awkward to bring it up at the daily in front of everyone

claim: Lena confirms discussing it with Nastya during a coffee break
quote: We talked about it separately with Nastya during a coffee break.

claim: Igor's reaction to the hidden deadline discussion
quote: I don't like that you two discussed it during a coffee break instead of telling me.

claim: Dima generalizes the silence into a team pattern
quote: we generally tend to hold back uncomfortable news until the last moment.

claim: Dima: the pattern repeated on a past project too
quote: That happened on the personal account project too.

claim: Dima calls Igor hard to reach on deadlines
quote: it felt like you weren't very open to discussing deadlines, your answer was always "we need to make it, we'll find a way."

claim: Igor disagrees with the closedness assessment
quote: Well the deadlines really were tight from above, I didn't make that up.

claim: External blocker: partner courier API down for 4 hours
quote: the partner's courier API was down for four hours on Wednesday of the second week, we couldn't test the integration all day.

claim: External blocker: legal changes to geolocation consent 3 days before release
quote: legal sent changes to the courier geolocation consent literally three days before release, we didn't make that one up either.

claim: Consequence of the legal blocker — Lena lost a day redrawing
quote: I had to redraw one consent screen on the fly, that ate up almost a whole day for me.

claim: Attribution for episode 1 (deadline slip): communication — Lena didn't raise the risk at the daily
quote: it felt awkward to bring it up at the daily in front of everyone

claim: Attribution for episode 1: Nastya also didn't voice the risk out loud
quote: I remember that Tuesday, I had a feeling we weren't going to make it, but I didn't say it out loud at the time.

claim: Attribution for episode 2 (handoff): process — Igor didn't include support in the plan
quote: That's my miss, I forgot to include Oleg in the plan early on, thought we'd have more time.

claim: Attribution for episode 3: process/resources — job failed with no alert
quote: the nightly slot recalculation job would sometimes fail silently, no alert

claim: Disagreement: Igor explains the slip via external pressure from legal
quote: a change from legal, I couldn't ignore it

claim: Disagreement: Igor says pressure came from above, not his own choice
quote: the deadlines really were tight from above, I didn't make that up

claim: Disagreement: Dima points to Igor's closedness to discussion
quote: you weren't very open to discussing deadlines

claim: Disagreement: Nastya admits her own silence
quote: I didn't say it out loud at the time

claim: Design-review success is called luck, not a systemic practice
quote: this time it happened by chance, we got lucky that I wasn't loaded up at that moment

claim: Facilitator notes the gap between the try item and the real reason for success
quote: flagging that Nastya herself says it was more luck with workload than a systemic practice

claim: Unresolved conflict: Igor is upset about the hidden deadline discussion
quote: I don't like that you two discussed it during a coffee break instead of telling me.

claim: Unresolved conflict: Dima calls it a team pattern instead of resolving it on the spot
quote: a pattern on the team that's worth discussing separately, not at this retro

claim: Facilitator defers resolving the conflict for later
quote: note it separately as an unresolved question about openness in discussing deadlines

claim: Priority: pre-launch checklist — has owner, deadline, group support
quote: I'll take it, I already have a draft from the last project, I'll just add three items.

claim: Contrast: alerts item is supported but has no owner
quote: everyone agrees it's important, but nobody's picking it up

claim: Main insight: root cause is late scope plus team silence, attribution diverges
quote: We only got the final feature scope in the second week of the sprint, even though the sprint was three weeks.
